_note: Updated by the workbench PO after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Define what 'good enough to charge for' looks like for the AI room assistant
priorities.note: Part of the orchestration Copilot readiness program. Measurable bar — not a feeling — for when Copilot can be part of a paid tier.
priorities.proposed_solution: Publish measurable Copilot commercial-readiness criteria (quality, safety, UX) that match the same founder decision as model benchmark timing and switch criteria.

priorities.title: Write user stories for the top three features in the backlog so engineering has clear success criteria
priorities.note: Features that enter development without user stories routinely get built to the wrong spec. Each of the top three backlog items needs a job-to-be-done framing and a measurable success metric before work starts.
priorities.proposed_solution: Write user stories with JTBD framing and a success metric for each of the top three backlog items before engineering starts.

priorities.title: Run a prioritization pass on the full backlog — the current order isn't justified
priorities.note: The backlog has grown without formal prioritization. The current sequence reflects what was added last, not what matters most to users.
priorities.proposed_solution: Re-prioritize the full backlog with explicit trade-offs and documented reasons for what moved down.

priorities.title: Document the onboarding flow — what does a new user do in their first session?
priorities.note: No one has defined the intended first-session experience. If we don't know what success looks like, we can't measure whether new users are reaching it.
priorities.proposed_solution: Document the first-session onboarding flow end-to-end so activation and drop-offs can be measured.

risks_p1.title: No defined bar for Copilot commercial readiness
risks_p1.note: Without a clear threshold, the paid launch will keep slipping. This is a strategic risk as much as a product risk.

founder_decisions.title: Copilot program — commercial bar, benchmark timing, and switch criteria
founder_decisions.note: Same founder decision as orchestration-status.json. PO publishes measurable criteria; founder signs once with engineering on when to run the model benchmark.

actions.name: Feature Prioritization
actions.description: Ranked backlog with explicit trade-off acknowledgment — what was deprioritized and why.
actions.trigger: Quarterly or when the backlog is stale or contested

actions.name: User Story Review
actions.description: User story with JTBD framing and a measurable success metric for a proposed feature — flag if none can be defined.
actions.trigger: Any proposed feature before it enters development

actions.name: Product Brief
actions.description: Full brief for an approved feature — user need, JTBD, success metric, scope boundary, out-of-scope items.
actions.trigger: A feature is approved and needs a spec before engineering begins

actions.name: Roadmap Health Check
actions.description: Sequence sanity check — does the current roadmap order serve a coherent product strategy?
